title: Scientific Professions
description: A collection of RAND research on the topic of Scientific Professions
text:
Mapping the Informal Bioeconomy: Assessing the Current Landscape and Opportunities for Reinforcing Biosafety and Biosecurity
The rapid growth of biotechnology research has resulted in a powerful ecosystem known as the bioeconomy. In this report, the authors seek to map and evaluate the informal bioeconomy from a biosecurity perspective.
Finding International Rockstar Researchers: Identifying Predictors of Success Following an International Move
After relocating internationally, some researchers thrive in their new environments, producing discoveries at rates higher than before their move. This report aims to identify predictors of successful outcomes after an international move.
Empowering Early Career Researchers to Tackle Global Challenges: The Frontiers Programme
The Royal Academy of Engineering's Frontiers Programme empowers early career researchers to address global challenges through interdisciplinary collaboration, equitable partnerships, and mentorship, enhancing the research-for-development landscape.
Evaluation of the Frontiers programme: Final evaluation report
RAND Europe was commissioned by the Royal Academy of Engineering to evaluate its Frontiers programme. This report assesses the programme's contributions, its added value and role in the wider ecosystem using a theory-based evaluation approach.
Evaluation of the UK Research Partnership Investment Fund (UKRPIF): Interim Findings
This infographic by RAND Europe for Research England summarises the interim findings of our evaluation of UKRPIF, including an overview of the programme. Key results centre around enhanced research facilities, leveraged income and partnerships.
Evaluation of the UK Research Partnership Investment Fund: Interim Report
This report by RAND Europe for Research England summarises the interim findings of our evaluation of UKRPIF, including an overview of the programme. Key results centre around enhanced research facilities, leveraged income and partnerships.
Evaluation of the UK Research Partnership Investment Fund: Evaluation Plan
This RAND Europe and Frontier Economics report for Research England outlines the funding landscape UKRPIF operates in, examines different approaches to evaluating investments in R&I infrastructure and outlines the plan for evaluating the programme.
Centers of Excellence Can Support Federal Programs' Mission and Enhance Engagement with Underserved Communities
Centers of Excellence, academic consortia that engage in research to support federal agencies, can help federal programs expand their geographic reach and increase the diversity of their technical workforce.
Evaluation of the Future Leaders Fellowships scheme: Process evaluation report
The process evaluation of the Future Leaders Fellowship concluded that, with some room for improvement, the scheme is in good health and has been delivered to the satisfaction of applicants, fellows and host organisations.
Demographic Diversity of the Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) Workforce in the U.S. Department of Defense: Analysis of Compensation and Employment Outcomes
The authors examine compensation differences for gender, racial, and ethnic groups in the Department of Defense civilian science, technology, engineering, and mathematics workforce and explore ways to address these differences.
International Basic Research Collaboration at the U.S. Department of Defense: An Overview
RAND National Defense Research Institute researchers studied how the U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) approaches international basic research collaboration (IBRC) and formulated suggestions for DoD to improve how it uses IBRC.
Pittsburgh Science and Technology Sector Is Large, but Faces Challenges; Improvements Needed to Continue to Flourish
The Pittsburgh region has a robust science and technology sector that is larger than the national average, but action is needed to safeguard the region's strengths and further its ability to compete with other technology hubs.
